##LOCALITY=Panasqueira mine, near Fundão, Portugal

##SOURCE=Royal Ontario Museum
##DESCRIPTION=Pale orange fragments, intimately associated with panasqueiraite

##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Mg_0.88_Fe^2+^_0.63_Mn^2+^_0.47_Ca_0.02_)(P_1.00_O_4_)(F_0.93_(OH)_0.07_) ; = light phase and the dark phase is panasqueiraite: (Ca_0.99_Mn_0.01_)(Mg_0.99_Fe_0.01_)P_1.00_O_4_(OH)  OH estimated by charge balance
